findings.message: MCP server binary path and working directory resolved from ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} env var; poisoned env var redirects MCP server execution
All 25 claude/agents/ stub agents lack an output format specification in their body; stubs defer to MCP tool get_agent but output contract is not specified
All 25 claude/agents/ stubs have zero standalone example blocks in document body; examples are embedded in YAML description field only and not surfaced as agent-body examples

All 25 claude/agents/ stubs defer methodology to MCP tool 'get_agent' declared in claude/.mcp.json; if CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is unset or MCP server unavailable, all stubs function as empty agents with no guidance
Five vague word instances: 'appropriate' twice in commentary and body, 'proper' twice in implementation standards and error handling guidance, 'necessary' in Output Contract

Five vague word instances: 'appropriate' twice in methodology (including 'appropriate denormalization'), 'proper' in indexing guidance, 'ensure' in query frequency check, 'necessary' in Output Contract
Four vague word instances: 'appropriate' twice in commentary and protocol section including 'appropriate RTS protocol', 'proper' in lock analysis, 'necessary' in Output Contract
Four vague word instances: 'appropriate' twice (example body and commentary), 'proper' in example body, 'necessary' in Output Contract boilerplate
Body has Methodology, Work Areas, and Constraints sections but no Output Format section; inconsistent with most other claude/src/agents/ which include an Output Contract
Body has Methodology, Work Areas, and Constraints sections but no Output Format section; inconsistent with most other claude/src/agents/
Body has Methodology, Work Areas, and Constraints sections but no Output Format section; inconsistent with most other claude/src/agents/
Four vague word instances: 'appropriate' in commentary, 'proper' twice in patterns and decision framework sections, 'necessary' in Output Contract boilerplate
No Output Format section in body; agent has Testing Standards and Test Types sections but omits any specification of how results or reports should be structured

Four vague word instances: 'appropriate' twice in methodology including 'appropriate to the task type' and 'appropriate interaction patterns', 'efficient' in decision table, 'necessary' in Output Contract
Hook command binary paths resolved from ${extensionPath} env var; attacker-controlled env var redirects hook execution to arbitrary binary
MAESTRO_RUNTIME env var selects platform adapter at runtime without validation; untrusted input could load unexpected or unintended adapter
prepare lifecycle script automatically runs node scripts/install-git-hooks.js on npm install, silently reconfiguring git core.hooksPath without user consent
devDependency c8 pinned with caret range (^10.1.3) allowing automatic minor and patch version upgrades on fresh installs
Five vague word instances: 'appropriate' in commentary, 'comprehensive' twice in persona and methodology sections, 'proper' in mocking guidance, 'relevant' in input type description
Script writes plugin files to ~/.codex/plugins/maestro/ and modifies ~/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json outside repository directory
execSync executes git subprocesses automatically via prepare lifecycle hook, running on every npm install in any consuming repository
Modifies git core.hooksPath to .githooks/ directory; if .githooks/ contents are malicious, all future git operations execute attacker code
Frontmatter lacks model field; all 75 agents in src/agents/, plugins/maestro/src/agents/, and claude/src/agents/ share this defect universally

Three parallel implementations exist for the same 15 core agent roles (src/agents/ at ~89, claude/src/agents/ at ~90, claude/agents/ stubs at 75); quality diverges 14 points between full-body and stub variants

Five vague word instances: 'appropriate' appears 4 times (2 in commentary, 2 in body including 'appropriate level of token granularity' and 'appropriate composition pattern') plus 'necessary' in Output Contract
